cursor_rules.title: Docker Best Practices for Java 25 Applications
cursor_rules.description: Comprehensive guidelines for containerizing Java 25 applications using Docker, focusing on performance optimization, security enhancements, and efficient resource utilization.

rule.title: Use Appropriate Base Images
rule.description: Select the most suitable base image for your Java 25 application, considering size, security, and compatibility requirements.

guidelines.guideline: Use `eclipse-temurin:25-jre-alpine` for minimal production images
Use `eclipse-temurin:25-jdk-alpine` only when JDK tools are required at runtime
Prefer Alpine Linux variants for smaller image sizes
Use specific version tags instead of `latest` for reproducible builds

rule.example:
# Production runtime image FROM eclipse-temurin:25-jre-alpine
# Development/build image FROM eclipse-temurin:25-jdk-alpine AS build

rule.title: Implement Multi-Stage Builds
rule.description: Separate the build environment from the runtime environment to create lean, secure final images by excluding unnecessary build tools and dependencies.

guidelines.guideline: Use a build stage with JDK for compilation
Use a runtime stage with JRE for execution
Copy only necessary artifacts between stages
Minimize the number of layers in the final image

rule.example:
# Build stage FROM eclipse-temurin:25-jdk-alpine AS build WORKDIR /app COPY pom.xml . COPY src ./src RUN ./mvnw clean package -DskipTests
# Runtime stage FROM eclipse-temurin:25-jre-alpine WORKDIR /app COPY --from=build /app/target/*.jar app.jar EXPOSE 8080 ENTRYPOINT ["java", "-jar", "app.jar"]

rule.title: Optimize JVM Configuration for Containers
rule.description: Configure the JVM to work efficiently within container resource constraints, taking advantage of Java 25's container awareness features.

guidelines.guideline: Use `-XX:+UseContainerSupport` to enable container awareness (enabled by default in Java 25)
Set appropriate heap size using `-Xms` and `-Xmx` based on container memory limits
Use `-XX:+UseG1GC` for better performance in containerized environments
Enable JVM ergonomics with `-XX:+UnlockExperimentalVMOptions` for Java 25 features
Use `-XX:+ExitOnOutOfMemoryError` to ensure container restart on OOM

rule.title: Implement Security Best Practices
rule.description: Enhance container security by following the principle of least privilege and implementing defense-in-depth strategies.

guidelines.guideline: Create and use a non-root user for running the application
Use read-only root filesystem when possible
Minimize the attack surface by removing unnecessary packages
Scan images for vulnerabilities regularly
Use secrets management for sensitive data

rule.example:
# Create non-root user RUN adduser --disabled-password --gecos "" --uid 1001 appuser
# Set ownership and permissions COPY --chown=appuser:appuser app.jar /app/app.jar
# Switch to non-root user USER appuser
# Use read-only filesystem # docker run --read-only --tmpfs /tmp myapp:latest

rule.title: Optimize Image Layers and Caching
rule.description: Structure Dockerfile commands to maximize Docker layer caching and minimize image size.

guidelines.guideline: Order commands from least to most frequently changing
Copy dependency files before source code to leverage cache
Combine related RUN commands to reduce layers
Use `.dockerignore` to exclude unnecessary files
Clean up package caches and temporary files in the same layer

rule.example:
# Copy dependency files first for better caching COPY pom.xml ./ COPY mvnw ./ COPY .mvn ./.mvn
# Download dependencies (cached layer) RUN ./mvnw dependency:resolve
# Copy source code (changes more frequently) COPY src ./src
# Build application RUN ./mvnw clean package -DskipTests && \ rm -rf ~/.m2/repository && \ rm -rf target/classes target/generated-sources

rule.title: Configure Health Checks and Monitoring
rule.description: Implement comprehensive health checking and monitoring to ensure application reliability and observability.

guidelines.guideline: Define Docker health checks for container orchestration
Implement application health endpoints
Configure proper logging to stdout/stderr
Use structured logging formats (JSON) for better parsing
Include JVM metrics and garbage collection monitoring

rule.example:
# Docker health check HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=60s --retries=3 \ CMD curl -f http://localhost:8080/actuator/health || exit 1
# Application health endpoint (Spring Boot example) # management.endpoints.web.exposure.include=health,metrics,info # management.endpoint.health.show-details=always
# JVM monitoring flags ENV JAVA_OPTS="$JAVA_OPTS -XX:+PrintGCDetails -XX:+PrintGCTimeStamps"

rule.title: Manage Configuration and Secrets
rule.description: Externalize configuration and handle secrets securely to support different deployment environments.

guidelines.guideline: Use environment variables for configuration
Support configuration files mounted as volumes
Never embed secrets in Docker images
Use Docker secrets or external secret management
Implement configuration validation at startup

rule.example:
# Environment-based configuration ENV SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE=production ENV SERVER_PORT=8080 ENV MANAGEMENT_PORT=9090
# Support for external configuration VOLUME ["/app/config"]
# Configuration precedence: env vars > mounted files > defaults ENTRYPOINT ["java", "-Dspring.config.location=classpath:/,file:/app/config/", "-jar", "app.jar"]

rule.title: Optimize for Different Deployment Scenarios
rule.description: Create flexible Docker configurations that work well across development, testing, and production environments.

guidelines.guideline: Use build arguments for environment-specific customization
Support both standalone and orchestrated deployments
Implement graceful shutdown handling
Configure appropriate resource limits and requests
Use init systems for proper signal handling in containers

rule.example:
# Build arguments for flexibility ARG JAVA_OPTS_BUILD="" ARG APP_VERSION="latest"
# Graceful shutdown support ENV JAVA_OPTS="$JAVA_OPTS -Dserver.shutdown=graceful"
# Signal handling for proper shutdown RUN apk add --no-cache tini ENTRYPOINT ["tini", "--"] CMD ["java", "-jar", "app.jar"]
# Resource limits in deployment # resources: # limits: # memory: "1Gi" # cpu: "1000m" # requests: # memory: "512Mi" # cpu: "500m"

rule.title: Leverage Java 25 Specific Features
rule.description: Take advantage of Java 25's latest features and improvements for better container performance and developer experience.

guidelines.guideline: Use Virtual Threads for improved scalability in I/O-intensive applications
Leverage Pattern Matching and Switch Expressions for cleaner code
Use Record Classes for data transfer objects
Take advantage of improved garbage collection algorithms
Use Text Blocks for embedded configuration or SQL

rule.title: Implement Efficient Development Workflow
rule.description: Create Docker configurations that support efficient development cycles and debugging capabilities.

guidelines.guideline: Use Docker Compose for local development environments
Implement hot-reload capabilities for development
Support remote debugging configurations
Create separate profiles for development and production
Use volume mounts for rapid iteration during development

rule.example:
# Development Dockerfile FROM eclipse-temurin:25-jdk-alpine AS development WORKDIR /app COPY pom.xml . RUN ./mvnw dependency:resolve VOLUME ["/app/src", "/app/target"] EXPOSE 8080 5005 ENV JAVA_OPTS="-agentlib:jdwp=transport=dt_socket,server=y,suspend=n,address=*:5005" CMD ["./mvnw", "spring-boot:run"]
# docker-compose.yml for development # version: '3.8' # services: # app: # build: # context: . # target: development # ports: # - "8080:8080" # - "5005:5005" # volumes: # - ./src:/app/src # - ./target:/app/target

anti_pattern.title: Using Root User in Production
anti_pattern.description: Running applications as root user increases security risks

anti_pattern.title: Installing Unnecessary Packages
anti_pattern.description: Including development tools and unnecessary packages in production images
anti_pattern.example: RUN apk add --no-cache curl wget vim git # AVOID UNNECESSARY TOOLS

anti_pattern.title: Hardcoding Configuration
anti_pattern.description: Embedding environment-specific configuration in Docker images

anti_pattern.title: Ignoring JVM Container Settings
anti_pattern.description: Not configuring JVM for container resource limits
anti_pattern.example: java -Xmx4g -jar app.jar # AVOID FIXED HEAP SIZES

anti_pattern.title: Using Latest Tags in Production
anti_pattern.description: Using unstable or latest tags for base images in production

best_practices_summary.practice: Use multi-stage builds with appropriate base images
Implement security best practices with non-root users
Optimize Docker layers and caching strategies
Configure comprehensive health checks and monitoring
Externalize configuration and manage secrets properly
Leverage Java 25 specific features and improvements
